Reduced distress has led to less demand for restructuring and recovery-optimization skill sets in recent years, but “it really feels like in the next four or five [years]... those characteristics are going to be important for performance in this space,” according to Miller. title_cn: 奇怪的批次：为什么石油和牛肉的价格毫无意义（播客）
summary: Whether it’s the price of a barrel of Brent crude or a pound of beef, it’s clear prices are skyrocketing for all kinds of goods and commodities. Price shocks and shortages are, if anything, the way consumers understand the economy right now — at the grocery store or at the gas pump. Certainly, current (and future) shocks can be explained by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. But the environment is weirder than just across the board price increases: The price of corn has barely moved, for insta

title: 'The Clock is Ticking' - Trump's Latest Iran Threat Pushes Oil Prices Higher
title_cn: “时间在流逝”——特朗普最新的伊朗威胁推高油价
summary:
US President Donald Trump expressed frustration with Iran and told it the "clock is ticking," hours after drones targeted a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates. The fresh threats helped push up oil prices further on Monday. Earlier on Sunday, a drone sparked a fire in a power station at the United Arab Emirates' Barakah nuclear plant, underscoring the fragility of the truce. Bloomberg's Joumanna Bercetche joined Caroline Hepker and Tiwa Adebayo to discuss 

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks, Bonds Decline as Brent Hits $110 | Bloomberg Brief 5/18/2026

summary: A global bond selloff eases while stocks decline amid rising oil prices. President Trump expresses frustration with Iran, saying "the clock is ticking" on making a deal. The White House says China has agreed to buy at least $17 billion of US agricultural products and establish boards of trade and investment after the summit between President Trump and President Xi. Sharon Bell of Goldman Sachs discusses the equity market as investors look ahead to Nvidia's earnings report later in the week. Jude Blanchette of the RAND China Research Center analyzes the outcomes of the Trump-Xi summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why Investors Should Expect Lower Returns From Here

Paolini argues that the AI investment boom, strong corporate earnings and persistent US economic strength continue to support markets — but warns that the era of US exceptionalism may be fading, making diversification and lower long-term return expectations essential for investors. (Source: Bloomberg)

title_cn: 中国的能源繁荣可能赋予其人工智能优势
summary: As the United States and China compete for dominance in artificial intelligence, energy is emerging as a critical battleground. Former Treasury Secretary Hank Paulson warns that while the US still leads in AI technology, electricity shortages could become a major constraint as data center demand surges. Former US Ambassador to China Nicholas Burns says China’s enormous investments in transmission, renewables, batteries, and power generation are already reshaping global supply chains, while Hoover Institution senior fellow Elizabeth Economy argues Beijing’s clean-energy strategy is as much about economic and geopolitical power as climate policy. Together, their assessments point to a growing reality: the AI race may depend not just on chips and software, but on generating enough power to sustain the technology. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Samsung, Labor Union to Meet in ‘Last Chance’ to Avert Strike
title_cn: 三星与工会将召开避免罢工的“最后机会”
summary: Samsung Electronics Co. and its largest labor union are set to resume negotiations Monday to avert a strike that the nation’s prime minister warned could wreak havoc on the economy.

title: ASML Partners With Tata Electronics to Advance India Chip Plans
title_cn: ASML 与塔塔电子合作推进印度芯片计划
summary: ASML Holding NV entered into a partnership agreement with Tata Electronics Private Limited aimed at ramping up India’s goal to develop domestic chip-manufacturing capabilities.

title: AI Poised to Tilt Job Market Leverage Toward Older Workers
title_cn: 人工智能将使就业市场的影响力向老年工人倾斜
summary: When it comes to job cuts, older workers are often disproportionately affected. But a new survey of chief executive officers suggests this won’t be a given as companies adopt artificial intelligence.

title: Iran Starts Bitcoin-Backed Shipping Insurance for Hormuz Strait
title_cn: 伊朗为霍尔木兹海峡启动比特币支持的航运保险
summary: Iran has started a Bitcoin-backed insurance service for shipping companies that want to transit the Strait of Hormuz, the semi-official Fars news agency reported, citing documents obtained from Iran’s Ministry of Economy and Financial Affairs.

title: Iranian Media Said US Offered Interim Waiver on Oil Sanctions
title_cn: 伊朗媒体称美国提出临时豁免石油制裁
summary: An Iranian media outlet reported that the US proposed a temporary waiver of sanctions on the Islamic Republic’s oil, a key demand by Tehran to agree to a peace deal and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Starmer Won’t Give a Timetable for Departure, Spokesman Says
title_cn: 发言人称斯塔默不会给出出发时间表
summary: Keir Starmer will not set out a plan for leaving office, his spokesman said as pressure mounts on Britain’s prime minister to step aside to end the leadership crisis engulfing the ruling Labour Party.

title: South Africa’s Top Court Limits State Role in Doctor Placements
title_cn: 南非最高法院限制国家在医生安置中的作用
summary: South Africa’s top court struck down parts of a law that would enable the government to determine where doctors and other healthcare professionals can work, because their constitutional rights would be undermined.

title: Bessent Urges G-7 to Increase Sanctions on Iran
title_cn: 贝森特敦促七国集团加大对伊朗的制裁力度
summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, speaking ahead of a meeting with other Group of Seven finance ministers in Paris, called on G-7 nations and other allies to increase sanctions on Iran in an effort to "crack down on the illicit finance that is fueling the Iranian war machine." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump’s Push to Punish GOP Dissenters Creates Risks for Party
title_cn: 特朗普推动惩罚共和党持不同政见者给共和党带来了风险
summary: The president’s emphasis on intraparty retribution opens new potential roadblocks

title: Cutthroat Texas Runoff Pits Two Trump Allies Against Each Other
title_cn: 残酷的德克萨斯决选让特朗普的两个盟友相互对立
summary: As John Cornyn closes out a cutthroat primary election that threatens to end his 24-year tenure in the US Senate, the Republican is aiming to leave voters with a simple message: He’s just as MAGA as the rest of the party faithful.

title: Hungary Central Bank Chief Says He Won’t Hinder Magyar Euro Plan
title_cn: 匈牙利央行行长表示不会阻碍马扎尔欧元计划
summary: Hungary’s central bank governor, Mihaly Varga, gave a cautious endorsement of the government’s plan to join the euro, but warned careful preparation would be needed.

title: EU Plans Tougher Trade Measures to Rebuff Chinese Export Surge
title_cn: 欧盟计划采取更严厉的贸易措施阻止中国出口激增
summary: European Union officials are exploring whether the bloc needs new measures to protect its economy against an influx of Chinese goods, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: US Strikes Kill Additional Islamic Militants in Northern Nigeria
title_cn: 美国空袭杀死尼日利亚北部更多伊斯兰武装分子
summary: Renewed US air strikes killed additional Islamist militants in Nigeria’s northern region, a day after a joint operation with the West African nation claimed the life of a senior ISIS commander.

title: Ebola Spread Shows Deadly Cost of Aid Retreat
title_cn: 埃博拉病毒的蔓延表明援助撤退的代价是致命的
summary: The Bundibugyo strain may have circulated undetected for weeks in northeastern Congo.

title: Starmer Revolt Revives Brexit Reversal Debate
title_cn: 斯塔默起义重燃英国脱欧逆转辩论
summary: The question of whether the UK should unwind a decade of estrangement from the European Union is resurfacing as the Labour Party starts to imagine a future beyond Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Bloomberg's Joe Mayes reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japan’s Takaichi U-Turns on Extra Budget After Mideast Shock
title_cn: 中东震荡后日本高市突然取消额外预算
summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi called for an extra budget in response to rising commodity prices driven by the ongoing Middle East conflict, backtracking on her insistence that such a measure wasn’t necessary and helping spark fresh record highs in super-long bond yields.

title: IEA Chief Warns Commercial Oil Inventories Are Falling Very Fast
title_cn: IEA负责人警告商业石油库存正在快速下降
summary: International Energy Agency Executive Director Fatih Birol warned that the tally of commercial oil inventories is shrinking at an accelerated pace.

title: Ukraine Says It’s Unsure of US Stance on Russia Oil Sanctions
title_cn: 乌克兰表示不确定美国对俄罗斯石油制裁的立场
summary: Ukraine remains uncertain about the US position on Russian oil, even after Washington let its waiver for purchases of Russian crude expire.

title: Lai Says Taiwan Won’t Be Sacrificed as Trump Weighs Arms Deal
title_cn: 赖清德表示，特朗普考虑武器交易时，台湾不会被牺牲
summary: President Lai Ching-te invoked Taiwan’s status as a global supplier of advanced tech like semiconductors as questions mount over US support for the democracy after the summit between President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

title: ICC-Wanted Senator Still in Philippines, Fellow Lawmaker Says
title_cn: 议员同事表示，国际刑事法院通缉的参议员仍在菲律宾
summary: Philippine Senator Ronald Dela Rosa, wanted by the International Criminal Court on allegations of crimes against humanity, is still in the country and has no plans to leave, according to a fellow senator.

title: Sara Duterte Trial to Open Next Phase of Philippine Political Turmoil
title_cn: 萨拉·杜特尔特审判将开启菲律宾下一阶段的政治动荡
summary: After a tumultuous week that started with a Senate power grab and descended into a terrifying night of gunfire, the impeachment saga against Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte moved Monday to the trial phase.

title: China’s Military Vows to Raise Trust With US After Xi-Trump Meet
title_cn: 习近平与特朗普会面后，中国军队誓言增强与美国的信任
summary: China’s Ministry of Defense signaled it’s open to building trust with Washington, following President Donald Trump’s meeting with Xi Jinping last week.

title: CDC Working to Get Some Americans Out of Ebola Region in Congo
title_cn: 疾病预防控制中心正在努力让一些美国人脱离刚果埃博拉地区
summary: US authorities are working to extract a small number of Americans from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, where a deadly Ebola outbreak is spreading.

title: Spain’s Sánchez Hurt by Record Defeat in Socialist Stronghold
title_cn: 西班牙桑切斯因在社会主义据点遭遇创纪录失败而受伤
summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez oversaw his party’s worst ever result in Andalusia’s regional election, dealing a blow to his hopes of pulling off a comeback victory in next year’s general election.

title: Hungarian President Sulyok Rejects Magyar’s Call to Resign
title_cn: 匈牙利总统苏廖克拒绝马扎尔的辞职呼吁
summary: Hungarian President Tamas Sulyok rejected demands from new Prime Minister Peter Magyar to resign by the end of May, setting the stage for a legal battle in Budapest.

title: Bessent Calls on G-7 Nations, Others to Increase Iran Sanctions
title_cn: 贝森特呼吁七国集团和其他国家加强对伊朗的制裁
summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent called on the G-7 nations and others to increase sanctions on Iran as the conflict that has snarled a large portion of the global energy trade drags on without an end in sight.

title: G-7 Ministers to Discuss Inflation, Imbalances, Oil
title_cn: 七国集团部长讨论通货膨胀、失衡和石油问题
summary: Group of Seven finance chiefs are meeting in Paris to discuss how oil-fueled inflation risks overshadowing economic prospects. The talks are meant to dwell on imbalances ranging from the US budget deficit to China’s weak consumption. Bloomberg's Oliver Crook reports from Paris. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lescure Says European Economy 'Proving Quite Resilient'
title_cn: Lescure表示欧洲经济“证明相当有弹性”
summary: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure discusses the European economy, the risk of inflation and rising bond yields. Speaking to Bloomberg's Oliver Crook on the sidelines of a meeting between Group of Seven finance ministers in Paris, he says the European economy is "proving quite resilient."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trumps Says Clock is Ticking for Iran | Daybreak Europe 05/18/2026
title_cn: 特朗普称伊朗的时间已经不多了欧洲黎明 05/18/2026
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

On today's show, the global bond selloff deepens as rising oil prices fuel inflation concerns. President Trump has warned Iran that the 'clock is ticking' for a deal to be done to end the war, but both sides remain at loggerheads.

Spiking bond yields are likely to dominate a meeting of G-7 finance ministers in Paris, as oil-fueled inflation threatens global growth. The French Finance Minister Roland Lescure tells Bloomberg the bond market is correcting rather than collapsing.

Today's guests: Seema Shah, Principal Asset Management, Chief Global Strategist; Roland Lescure, French Finance Minister. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Revolt Against Starmer Revives UK Debate Over Undoing Brexit
title_cn: 对斯塔默的反抗重新引发了英国关于取消脱欧的辩论
summary: The thorny question of whether Britain should unwind a decade of estrangement from the European Union is resurfacing as the Labour Party starts to imagine a future beyond Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

title: Anwar Floats Snap Malaysia Poll as Coalition Tensions Deepen
title_cn: 随着联盟紧张局势加深，安瓦尔浮出水面
summary: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim raised the prospect of a snap general election as jockeying within the country’s ruling alliance further tested his weakening position and raised questions about the country’s political stability.
